name: Word2Vec
parameter_count: Depends on vocabulary size and embedding dimension; typically ~300M for standard configurations
training_approach: Self-supervised learning using either Skip-gram (predict context from word) or CBOW (predict word from context) on large text corpora
key_innovations: Demonstrated that distributed word representations capture semantic relationships as vector arithmetic (king - man + woman = queen)
Showed that simple shallow networks trained on massive data produce rich semantic embeddings
Skip-gram with negative sampling made training efficient enough for billion-word corpora
Established word embeddings as the standard first layer of NLP systems for years

benchmark_highlights.word_analogy: Demonstrated semantic and syntactic analogies via vector arithmetic

lineage_path: Distributional Semantics -> Neural Language Models (Bengio 2003) -> Word2Vec -> GloVe -> ELMo -> BERT embeddings
cultural_impact: Word2Vec transformed NLP by showing that word meanings could be captured as dense vectors with remarkable algebraic properties. The 'king - man + woman = queen' example became one of the most widely cited demonstrations in AI, making word embeddings intuitive to researchers and the public. Word2Vec laid the conceptual groundwork for the embedding-based approaches that underpin all modern LLMs.
